name: LoadRunnerEnterpriseSync
description: Scan a git repository for performance test script folders, compress each into a zip, and upload them to an OpenText Enterprise Performance Engineering server.
author: OpenText

inputs.name: descriptionString
inputs.label: Description
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional description for this task step.

inputs.name: varPCServer
inputs.label: Server and port
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter the hostname or IP address and port of the Enterprise Performance Engineering server in the format: http(s)://(Server):(port#). If you are required to use a tenant, use the following format: http(s)://(Server):(port#)/?tenant=(guid#). For example: https://MyServer.MyCompanyDomain.com:444/?tenant=fa128c06-5436-413d-9cfa-9f04bb738df3

inputs.name: varUseTokenForAuthentication
inputs.label: Authenticate with token
inputs.helpMarkDown: Turn on this option to use a token for authentication. This option is the only way of authentication for Enterprise Performance Engineering servers configured with SSO.

inputs.name: varUserName
inputs.label: User name \ Id key
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter the user name or the Id key of your token to connect to the server.

inputs.name: varPassWord
inputs.label: Password \ Secret key
inputs.helpMarkDown:
Enter the password or Secret key of your token to connect to the server.

Note: You can create a variable of type secret in the CI build to store the password, and specify the variable to avoid exposing a password in the build configuration. For example, '$(PCPassword)').

inputs.name: varDomain
inputs.label: Domain
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter the domain in which the project is stored.

inputs.name: varProject
inputs.label: Project
inputs.helpMarkDown: Enter the name of the project to which scripts are uploaded.

inputs.name: varWorkspaceDir
inputs.label: Workspace directory
inputs.helpMarkDown: Path to the root of the git repository to scan for performance test scripts. Defaults to the pipeline's source directory.

inputs.name: varBaseCommitSha
inputs.label: Base commit SHA (differential sync)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Optional. Git commit SHA to use as the baseline for differential sync. When provided, only script folders containing files changed since this commit are uploaded. Leave empty to upload all scripts (full sync). Typically set to the SHA stored from the previous successful build.

inputs.name: varRuntimeOnly
inputs.label: Runtime only
inputs.helpMarkDown: Upload scripts as runtime-only (cannot be edited in the Enterprise Performance Engineering UI). Equivalent to the 'runtimeOnly' option in the Enterprise Performance Engineering REST API.

inputs.name: varParallelUploads
inputs.label: Parallel uploads
inputs.helpMarkDown: Number of scripts to upload simultaneously (1–20). Default: 1 (sequential). Increase only when the target Enterprise Performance Engineering server supports concurrent uploads.

inputs.name: varSuccessThreshold
inputs.label: Success threshold (%)
inputs.helpMarkDown: Minimum percentage of scripts that must upload successfully for the task to pass (0–100). Leave empty to use the default (50%). If set to 0, the task passes even if no script uploads succeed (authentication failure still fails the task). If set to 100, the task fails if even one script fails to upload. Values outside 0–100 fall back to the default (50%). Note: 5 consecutive upload failures always abort the task with failure regardless of this setting.

inputs.name: varProxyUrl
inputs.label: Proxy URL
inputs.helpMarkDown: Add your local proxy in the format http(s)://(host):(port#) or leave empty if not using a local proxy.

inputs.name: varProxyUser
inputs.label: Proxy user
inputs.helpMarkDown: If you use a proxy requiring credentials, enter the user name.

inputs.name: varProxyPassword
inputs.label: Proxy password
inputs.helpMarkDown:
If you use a proxy requiring credentials, enter the password.

Note: You can create a variable of type secret in the CI build to store the password.

inputs.name: varArtifactsDir
inputs.label: Artifacts directory
inputs.helpMarkDown: Path to the directory where sync log files are stored.
